latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

As previously announced, realisation of assets within the Company's portfolio since the end of June, together with the announcement of a distribution to shareholders of $18.99mln being equivalent to $0.13 (13 cents) a share, result in the June 30 NAV being significantly different to the current position. Therefore, in addition to publishing the June 30 NAV, the Board has taken the decision to provide an indicative NAV as at September 30 that incorporates changes to the underlying portlio over the past quarter.
The Board estimates that as at the close of business on 30 September 2025, the indicative Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.0365
154% - see Note
Note: the indicative quarterly performance for Sep 30 comprises the distribution to shareholders plus a slight uplift in the underlying portfolio value.
An analysis of the portfolio as at June 30 and September 30 2025 is provided in the quarterly factsheet which is now available on the Company's website, please see the link below.
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/fact-sheets-and-fund-updates/2025/
Please note that the fact sheet will contain the detailed portfolio update, the Board's observations and conclusions as indicated in the previous shareholder notification of September 22nd.
The final September NAV will be published in due course.

latest.title: Receipt of Funds and Return of Capital
latest.content:
22 Sep 2025 17:20
RNS Number : 3167A
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
22 September 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
22 September 2025
RECEIPT OF FUNDS AND RETURN OF CAPITAL
The Board of Directors of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited is pleased to announce that the Company has received a distribution of US$2.7 million from the V Invest RJ Fund and US$18.59 million from Vision FCVS RJ Fund.
It has been resolved to return an amount of US$13 cents per share ($0.13) to shareholders, being US$18.99 million in total, based on the current number of shares in issue. This return of capital will be effected by way of an issue of redeemable B shares to existing shareholders
pro-rata
to their shareholding on the record date set out below and the subsequent redemption and cancellation of those B Shares.
At the next board meeting (8 October 2025) the Board and the Investment Advisor will discuss the remaining holdings in the portfolio, the potential outcomes and the estimated timing of their monetization. The Investment Advisor will then prepare a detailed shareholder report which will be released via the Exchange, detailing the discussion and the Board's recommendation to shareholders with regards to the Company's future.
It is expected that the timetable for the return of capital will be as follows:
25 September 2025
Ex date
26 September 2025
Record date for shareholders on the register to receive B shares
29 September 2025
B shares allotted and issued to shareholders on the register as at the Record date
30 September 2025
B shares redeemed and cancelled
31 October 2025
Redemption payment made
All redemption payments will be paid gross of tax.
Enquiries
Tim Gardner, Hindsight Solutions Limited
Tel. +44 7917 630 460
Email:
timgardner@hindsightsolutions.co.uk
Website:
​
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/
Apex Fund and Corporate Services (Guernsey) Limited
Telephone: +44 (0) 20 3530 3600

company_30s.what_they_do: Allanfield Group plc is a small‑cap UK company that develops, owns and manages residential and mixed‑use property assets. It focuses on acquiring land, constructing new homes or refurbishing existing buildings, and then leasing or selling them.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The UK housing market still has pockets of demand for affordable and mid‑range dwellings. Allanfield’s niche is to target areas with limited supply and modest growth, aiming for steady rental income or capital appreciation. Its ability to execute projects on a tight budget can give it an edge over larger developers that are burdened by higher overheads.
company_30s.current_state: The company is currently in a consolidation phase, having recently completed an AGM, released a net‑asset‑value fact sheet and announced a return of capital. These moves suggest it is trimming its balance sheet and focusing on core assets. The share price sits at the very low end of its valuation range, trading close to a fraction of a penny, indicating a steep decline from its peak.

rns_narrative: 1️⃣ Corporate governance and financial health: The AGM and NAV fact sheet show the board is keeping shareholders informed and the company is maintaining a modest cash position.
2️⃣ Capital management: The receipt of funds and subsequent return of capital signal that the firm is liquidating excess cash or paying dividends to keep the balance sheet lean.
3️⃣ Portfolio activity: Updates on holdings and portfolio changes hint at a gradual real‑estate optimisation strategy, possibly winding down under‑performing assets.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silence with a hint of cautious curiosity – the community is watching but not talking.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No active bullish chatter exists; the few older posts are neutral or mildly positive, reflecting historical optimism but not current enthusiasm.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: The lack of recent discussion is itself a bearish signal – investors are likely wary of the company’s limited upside and uncertain growth prospects.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Over the last year the tone has stayed flat; there’s no clear shift toward optimism or pessimism, keeping the market in a holding pattern.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public interest spikes, which is a good sign for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: A fresh development proposal or land acquisition announcement that could unlock new revenue streams.
A positive cash‑flow report or a dividend announcement that signals financial health.
A surge in trading volume that exceeds the typical daily average, indicating new institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to confirm a rebound are: (1) a jump in daily volume to 1.5–2× the mean, (2) a price bounce to the 20‑day moving average, and (3) a credible press release about a new project or partnership.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share appears to be in the late stages of a crash‑recovery cycle – the price is near its lowest support and the market has largely priced in the downside. A mean‑reversion move would need a clear catalyst and a sustained volume uptick.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is trading far below its historical valuation, and the market has largely ignored it. If a new development or capital injection materialises, the price could climb to the midpoint of its former range, delivering a 4–5× return. The asymmetry is favourable: the downside is capped at the current price, while the upside is potentially multi‑fold.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company fails to secure new projects or if it continues to return capital without generating income, the price may stagnate or slide further. The worst‑case scenario is a continued decline to a fraction of a penny, wiping out the investment.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 4–5× the current price. Potential downside: limited to the present value of the share. Risk‑reward ratio is roughly 4:1, which is attractive for a contrarian strategy.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed volume spike and a price bounce above the 20‑day average. Size the position to risk only 1–2% of capital per trade and place a stop just below the recent low. Use a trailing stop to lock in gains as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Watch for a press release that announces a new property or a partnership with a local developer. If the share price moves above the 50‑day moving average while volume remains above average, consider a small entry to test the thesis.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a delisting, filing for bankruptcy, or a material fraud claim, exit immediately. No other terminal risk is apparent at this time.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Allanfield is a silent, deeply discounted property developer that could surprise the market if a new project or capital injection sparks a bounce, offering a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian play.
